title: Burgers flux difference vanishes on constant states

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant grid state, the local flux difference of the Burgers flux is zero at every index.
assumptions: i is a natural-number grid index
the grid state is constant with value c
informal.expected_conclusion: The flux difference of a constant Burgers flux is zero.

declarations.name: burgersFlux
declarations.statement: def burgersFlux (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := (u i) ^ 2 / 2

declarations.name: burgersFlux_const_difference_zero
declarations.statement: theorem burgersFlux_const_difference_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (burgersFlux (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For a constant grid state, the local flux difference of the Burgers flux is zero at every index.
tasks.target: theorem burgersFlux_const_difference_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (burgersFlux (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding fluxDifference and burgersFlux, then using ring.

tasks.input: Find the generic definition used for local finite-volume flux differences.
